Name: Ducks, Geese, Ice, Water & Seasonal Transitions
description:
"Although I’m familiar with this area and have walked here before as well as eaten on the deck of Royal Sonesta which has a similar view of Boston, on this awe walk I really noticed what was very close to me i.e. the water/ice and ducks/geese which I usually don’t notice so much since normally I’m admiring the Boston buildings/skyline. It was an interesting time to be walking at the end of winter. I observed how much of the river was liquid water but that parts were also fragile-looking clear crystal like sheets of ice and then some thicker opaque white ice. It was interesting to see the ducks and geese swim through the liquid water but then also walk along the more solid ice. Sadly there was one goose who was dead and frozen/encased in the ice. And then alongside the river, there were geese walking and looking around in the grass/mud/puddles. In one sense the landscape was a bit drab but also nice to pause for a moment to notice the dirty snow, emerging grass, and mud puddles since I imagine in a couple weeks the landscape will look very different. I noticed various animal footprints as I crossed over a snowy area on my walk. In the background, I saw the Boston skyline and red line train passing by as usual.

I heard a variety of sounds including people’s footsteps running by, sirens, horns, construction, ducks/geese noises, the crunch of my boots walking over the snow at times."
